hooks.text: Your bank promised you a good rate. They kept that promise. It was a good rate. For them, not for you.
When your bank says we'll give you a good rate, there is one word in that sentence doing all the lying. It is not the word you think.
The bank's good rate was 7.05. The real rate banks trade at that same minute was 7.22. That gap quietly costs you 240 dollars on every 10,000.
A business owner heard good rate and felt safe. He never checked the rate banks actually use with each other. Two percent of every payment was already gone.
Good rate has no number behind it. It is a word, not a benchmark. And every payment you make on that word leaks money you will never see again.
